body:0002.text: The navigation bar is the menu displayed at the top of every page on your platform. It helps users quickly access key sections like the homepage, project pages, About page, and any custom pages you've added.

body:0004.text: Makes the platform easier to navigate

body:0005.text: Highlights what matters most to your community

body:0006.text: Reflects your organization's structure or priorities

body:0007.text: You can customize the navigation bar by changing the order of items, renaming pages, and linking projects to it.

body:0010.text: A custom page is a standalone, flexible page for sharing information with your community. It's useful for:

body:0017.text: Rich content (text, images, videos, buttons, links)

body:0018.text: Linked projects (filtered by tag or area)

body:0019.text: Attachments such as documents or reports

body:0020.text: You can also add custom pages to the navigation bar for easy access.

body:0021.text: Differences between a custom page and a folder/project

body:0022.text: Projects hold only their own project content.

body:0023.text: Folders can contain multiple projects and give you more flexibility in how you organize related content.

body:0024.text: Custom pages standalone pages that purpose serving custom content, not organize or group items.

body:0026.text: If you want a dedicated item in your navigation bar and need to group several projects together under it, we recommend using a folder. Folders can be easily customized and linked directly in the navigation bar.

body:0027.text: ℹ️ To learn how to add a folder to your navigation bar, see the section "How to add pages, projects or folders to the navigation bar." For a detailed guide on organizing content with folders, check out our support article Creating and Managing Folders.

body:0028.text: How to create and edit a custom page?

body:0032.text: Page title – the main heading on the page

body:0033.text: Navigation title – the name shown in the navigation bar

body:0036.text: After creating the page, you can edit the following sections:

body:0037.text: Hero banner: Add an image or header visual

body:0038.text: Top info section: Add formatted text, images, videos, buttons, or links

body:0039.text: Bottom info section: Add attachments or rich content

body:0040.text: Turn sections on or off as needed

body:0041.text: How to add projects/folders to a custom page

body:0042.text: You can link project/folder to a custom page based on tags or areas.

body:0043.text: Linked projects appear on the right-hand side of the page

body:0044.text: Here are the steps to link project/folder to a custom page:

body:0045.text: Make sure all projects/folder you wish to link have the correct tag or area:

body:0046.text: Tag: General project setting tab > scroll down to Tags section

body:0047.text: Area: General project setting tab > scoll down to Area filter section > select Selection > add the area

body:0048.text: In the custom page settings, in the Linked projects option By tag or By Area and select the tag/area you wish to use. You can select multiple tags/areas.

body:0049.text: How to add pages, projects or folder to the navigation bar?

body:0051.text: Add the top right click Add project or folders to navbar or Create custom page to create a new page

body:0053.text: Click Add to navbar to show a page in the navigation bar

body:0054.text: Click Remove from navbar to hide it

body:0055.text: Click Add project or folder to navbar to include a project or folder (with a custom name)

body:0056.text: Drag and drop items to reorder them

body:0058.text: The navigation bar can show up to 7 items

body:0059.text: Home is the only fixed item and cannot be removed or reordered

body:0060.text: All projects can be removed and reordered freely (note: this refers to the navigation bar item itself, not the projects in the dropdown-list)

body:0062.text: Projects in the navigation bar are user-specific, based on access rights

body:0064.text: How to customize the About page

body:0072.text: Contact details (e.g., an email for user questions)
